75464.subject: Weekly Recap: Saylor Starts Selling as Wall Street Races Onchain
75464.body:
**Happy 4th of July, Defiers!**

**Michael Saylor** spent the week doing the one thing he swore he never would. **Strategy** authorized its first real bitcoin sales to fund its preferred dividend, breaking the never-sell doctrine, and a Nasdaq-listed **AVAX** treasury vehicle warned it may not survive the year. The leverage trade that defined the last cycle is unwinding.

At the same time, Wall Street raced onchain. **Robinhood** opened its own layer-2 with tokenized stocks; **Securitize**, the engine behind BlackRock's BUIDL, went public and tokenized its own shares, and a 140-company consortium launched **Open USD** to take on Circle.

Enjoy!

———————————————————————————



View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/227ad23e-57ea-4aef-8a60-4f397ef55c8c/image.png?t=1780156424)
Caption: 

The most decentralized staking protocol just got a big upgrade. Saturn One improves Rocket Pool's capital efficiency, helping node operators stake with less ETH while increasing capacity for liquid stakers. **[Now is the best time to stake your ETH with Rocket Pool](https://rocketpool.net/?utm_source=defiant&utm_medium=newsletter&utm_campaign=weekly-recap)**.

———————————————————————————

———————————————————————————

##### WATCH

## Is ENS DAO Dead? Nick Johnson Faces His Critics

Youtube: 🟢 LIVE - Is ENS DAO Dead? Nick Johnson Faces His Critics (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=0XKnhfLugL4)

The week's governance flashpoint, live. ENS co-founder **Nick Johnson** used his voting power to move the DAO's treasury under a foundation; delegate **Alex Netto**, who calls the plan _"the downfall of ENS,"_ [debates him live](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=0XKnhfLugL4), with delegate James Waugh joining after. **Camila Russo** hosts.

———————————————————————————

# Top Stories This Week

## Saylor Ends Strategy's Never-Sell Era With $1.25B in Bitcoin Sales

**Strategy**'s board [authorized selling up to $1.25 billion of bitcoin](https://thedefiant.io/news/cefi/strategy-authorizes-up-to-usd1-25b-of-bitcoin-sales-as-saylor-formalizes-capital-pivot) to fund its STRC preferred dividend and buybacks, formally ending the never-sell doctrine Michael Saylor built the treasury around. The move followed the enterprise mNAV falling below 1; by Thursday, CEO Phong Le was personally buying the battered STRC preferred.

## Robinhood Takes Its Brokerage Onchain With Robinhood Chain

**Robinhood** [opened its Arbitrum-based Robinhood Chain](https://thedefiant.io/news/blockchains/robinhood-launches-robinhood-chain-mainnet-adds-stock-tokens-onchain-lending-and-agentic-crypto) to the public at a London keynote, rolling out tokenized stocks, a Morpho-powered lending product, and agentic crypto trading, plus expansions in Canada, Singapore, and the UK. The retail brokerage is converting its user base into an onchain distribution channel — and dYdX is already building a D

75463.subject: “Trophy wives” are out of fashion
75463.body:
<link rel=stylesheet href=https://marber-cdn.economist.com/foundations/latest/css/font-face.css>
 The Economist

 
 


 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5Mjd9AAcAAAAAA46s8saMcjxqAG_RisLDBbcEXaWYtDMamIjFplwv31KLo0Vda-ZY8fC54M13TiDcTXLhddsq8Si-IQOw9zQX7ypraDLfZiW8m1uBPdtuec9u 

July 4th 2026

 

 
 
 
The Economist This Weekend

 


 
 
 

 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5Mjd9AAcAAAAAA46s8saWWUOrcoXP4BjbjpXuT8UliYjNUdb8JLM0YWUedmfZ43Y9SZ0BsJRCeBK5_zhADxKAjFob6aKpMdouQmNW_fXbBvDAVzbMDZ8Zgvt2 

https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5Mjd9AAcAAAAAA46s8saYjMQQVXSqafhu60lv9vbFvK09TDbFw-4-Wrlfgb2EJntgEj7MBWiF3KGP0u96lMI0NLjuZpvkt61B5nIX-6EOzUQyCD5tadfcpSIu 


 


https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5Mjd9AAcAAAAAA46s8saa6AFm67eQxXEjZY3Rb__Gga1-_pc9e0F0CA4FzjerljKBOyj7oaNN9X_rt_G8UcYacW24EnPEAycmtBGYBc0dpSzIcJ5DkLs 



Doug Dowson

Data journalist



Wealthy people live differently from the rest of us. Their homes are bigger, their cars are shinier and their clothes are more stylish. And historically their partners have been younger, too.


Growing up in the 1990s I saw business tycoons such as Hugh Hefner and Hollywood stars such as Michael Douglas paired with women decades younger than them. Back then, it seemed normal for men of a certain age and status to marry “trophy wives”.


But this phenomenon 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5Mjd9AAcAAAAAA46s8sabmeuMffr7R41Ohas4o34Cw3AHaGGXkJAptuDwDq00ryofe8j4adoFwQZvyp9ZmT1hkkRy

75382.subject: Important updates to your MetaMask CL Card agreements
75382.body:
Important Update Regarding Your Card 
Dear Oleh

We’re reaching out to let you know about a small, behind-the-scenes update to 
the providers that support your MetaMask CL Card. First, the good news: there’s 
nothing you need to do. Your card and the way you use your account all stay 
exactly the same. We just believe you should always know who’s looking after 
your account. Here’s what’s changing, and why:

From 1 July 2026, the crypto-asset services in your account will be powered by 
MoonPay Europe B.V., a crypto-asset service provider authorized under the EU 
Markets in Crypto-Assets Regulation (MiCA) by the Netherlands Authority for the 
Financial Markets (AFM). Your card and payments providers continue unchanged.

This change does not alter the terms of your agreement with us. The updated 
terms that apply to the crypto-asset services are MoonPay’sTerms of Use 
<https://www.moonpay.com/legal/terms_of_use_europe> and Privacy Policy 
<https://www.moonpay.com/legal/privacy_policy>.

We’re proud to keep powering your MetaMask CL Card.

Questions about this update, or anything else on your account? We’re always 
happy to help at metamask@cl-cards.com <mailto:support@cl-cards.com>.

Need a new card? You can order one in the app in a couple of taps, and don’t 
forget you can delegate and spend straight from your wallet.

Crypto-asset services are provided by MoonPay Europe B.V., authorized by the 
Netherlands Authority for the Financial Markets
(AFM) under Regulation (EU) 2023/1114 (MiCA). Card and payment services are 
provided by Monavate Ltd, The Officers Mess Business Centre, Royston Road, 
Duxford,Cambridge, England, CB22 4QH
Unsubscribe 
<https://links.cl-cards.com/s/u/SiQ5AD4WG0u6jZlbDv-szh-il9QkEQ71njmDn-dBiCUfN6hxuhb3aQxuoEfI_4dCx_SaHmZg7ZrymRTUDsQfQzJYmGJsICucyuDvBin9LDGcybkapoMmbeTeXCMf11u3MeAA1EK7aQ6BoHaZQv3BRz5n8kQlSQt9aZ6ndM0DQE9t0VKBIW7P_5ZpMv6n/uQhGfkvsprPzLWOPljn1rZz5zHOjmEC5/22>
